assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised intragroup receivable denominated in a foreign currency or otherwise exposing the group to foreign exchange risk.

approaches.label: General hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements for hedged items and specifically addresses when exposures can be designated in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRIC 16 guidance on hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Ordinary accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context distinguishes hedge accounting from normal IFRS 9 accounting, under which gains and losses on financial instruments are recognised in profit or loss unless special hedge or OCI rules apply.
